items.title: Satellite IoT Contenders Are Racing Against a 6G Deadline
items.summary: The decades-old “ satellite-IoT” race , in which Internet of Things devices connect to satellites in orbit, has reached an inflection point in 2026. Satellite-IoT is growing in response to a rising need for connected devices in applications including livestock tracking , wildfire detection , global supply-chain...

items.title: Direct-to-Cell Technology: Enabling Satellite Connectivity for Legacy Devices
items.summary: Direct-to-cell technology uses LEO satellites as spaceborne cell towers. It delivers LTE services to existing smartphones without hardware changes, bridging global coverage gaps. What Attendees will Learn How DTC works as a spaceborne cell tower — LEO satellites carry LTE eNodeB payloads in regenerative mode. How they...

items.title: Starlink and Amazon Could Gain Access to Europe’s Satellite Airwaves
items.summary: The European Union plans to reserve most future satellite spectrum for regional firms while still allowing Starlink and Amazon to compete, reflecting Brussels' attempt to balance technological sovereignty with market openness in a strategically sensitive communications sector.

items.title: Inseego boosts its FWA team with 2 ex-Nokia execs
items.summary: Inseego pulled in two ex-Nokia execs for its FWA team and expanded the CCO's coverage to lead the Americas, including Latin America.

items.title: FCC tees up big auction for C-band ‘super band’
items.summary: The industry just finished a little AWS-3 auction and now it’s gearing up for a much bigger one: 160 megahertz in the upper C-band next year.

items.title: FCC scheme would approve satellite licenses in assembly-line process
items.summary: The goal of the Space Modernization Order is to turn the approval of applications, for both satellites and earth stations, into an “assembly line process,” according to FCC Chairman Brendan Carr.

items.title: Rakuten Symphony taps Amit Bhardwaj as Head of RAN Business Unit
items.summary: Bhardwaj will help drive the continued evolution and deployment of cloud-native, open RAN solutions for operators worldwide.

items.title: T-Mobile to shut down 2G GSM network this summer
items.summary: It’s hard to believe T-Mobile still operates a 2G GSM network, but it’s true – although not for long.

items.title: Now SpaceX is talking to Charter about a mobile offer: report
items.summary: The latest buzz is that Elon Musk’s SpaceX held executive-level talks with Charter Communications about partnering on a consumer mobile phone offering, per a Friday Bloomberg report.

items.title: Telcos want autonomous networks, but Ericsson says trust remains key hurdle
items.summary: Ericsson says telcos can’t automate their way into true network autonomy until they solve the harder problem: trusting AI to run the network without humans in the loop.

items.title: Will there be an AI cost reckoning for telcos? Here’s what AWS’ telco chief thinks.
items.summary: Telcos are eager to unleash AI on their networks, but AWS’ Jan Hofmeyr warns they could face a costly wake-up call if they mistake generic LLMs for a network-ready fix.

items.title: What’s the status of Boost Mobile cell phone service?
items.summary: We have the status of the Boost Mobile cell phone service now that Dish Wireless and Dish DBS filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on Tuesday.

items.title: Huawei and partners launch OpenAN project for Level 4 autonomous networks
items.summary: The project delivers a set of out-of-the-box open-source components targeting the accelerated delivery of smart link, agile build, large-scale collaboration and deterministic execution of telecom agents.

items.title: CoreWeave launches ARIA to boost AI-enabled research
items.summary: ARIA is an AI research agent that reads experiment data, uncovers hidden insights, and drives continuous model and agent improvement.

items.title: Update: Verizon, BT merge international enterprise operations in 50:50 JV worth $4B
items.summary: The JV will create a new global connectivity company focused on multinational organizations and serve 3,000+ businesses.
